questions.question: Explain the meaning of elections.
questions.answer: Elections are the process through which people choose their representatives to form the government in a democracy.

questions.question: Explain why elections are necessary in a democracy.
questions.answer: Elections allow citizens to choose their leaders and ensure that the government represents the will of the people.

questions.question: Explain political competition.
questions.answer: Political competition refers to the contest among political parties and candidates to win elections and form the government.

questions.question: Explain two advantages of political competition.
questions.answer: Political competition encourages leaders to serve people better and provides voters with choices among candidates.

questions.question: Explain the concept of electoral constituencies.
questions.answer: An electoral constituency is a geographical area from which voters elect one representative to the legislature.

questions.question: Explain the importance of constituencies in elections.
questions.answer: Constituencies divide the country into areas so that each area can elect one representative, ensuring fair representation.

questions.question: Explain universal adult franchise.
questions.answer: Universal adult franchise means every citizen aged 18 years or above has the right to vote in elections.

questions.question: Explain the voters list.
questions.answer: The voters list is an official list containing the names of all eligible voters in a constituency.

questions.question: Explain reserved constituencies.
questions.answer: Reserved constituencies are electoral areas where only candidates from Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes can contest elections.

questions.question: Explain the election campaign.
questions.answer: An election campaign is a period when candidates and political parties present their ideas and policies to voters.

questions.question: Explain the role of election symbols.
questions.answer: Election symbols help voters identify political parties and candidates easily.

questions.question: Explain the process of polling.
questions.answer: Polling is the process where voters cast their votes at polling booths using voting machines.

questions.question: Explain the process of vote counting.
questions.answer: Votes are counted after polling and the candidate with the highest number of votes is declared the winner.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Election Commission.
questions.answer: The Election Commission conducts elections and ensures they are free and fair.

questions.question: Explain why the Election Commission is independent.
questions.answer: It works independently from government control and ensures fair elections.

questions.question: Explain free and fair elections.
questions.answer: Free and fair elections allow voters to vote without pressure or manipulation.

questions.question: Explain voter turnout.
questions.answer: Voter turnout refers to the percentage of eligible voters who actually vote in elections.

questions.question: Explain electoral malpractice.
questions.answer: Electoral malpractice includes unfair practices such as bribery, intimidation or vote buying.

questions.question: Explain the importance of elections in accountability.
questions.answer: Elections ensure leaders remain accountable because voters can replace them if they fail to perform well.

questions.question: Explain the role of voters in democracy.
questions.answer: Voters participate in elections and decide who will govern the country.

questions.question: Explain the importance of election campaigns.
questions.answer: Election campaigns help voters understand candidates' policies and make informed decisions.

questions.question: Explain how elections strengthen democracy.
questions.answer: Elections strengthen democracy by allowing citizens to participate in governance.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a by-election.
questions.answer: A by-election is held to fill a vacant seat in a legislature.

questions.question: Explain the concept of a general election.
questions.answer: A general election is conducted to elect representatives for all constituencies.

questions.question: Explain the significance of transparency in elections.
questions.answer: Transparency ensures fairness and builds trust in democratic processes.
